title: Code completion settings
text: Code completion settings When you start typing in the code editor, the IDE displays a small pop-up list containing statements, functions, or pre-compiler directives. When you start typing with a character between a..z, the pop-up list displays statements. If you start typing with a ‘$’ character, the next character you type displays Uniface functions starting with the character you just typed. If you type a ‘#’ followed by a character, the editor displays the pre-compiler directives. The code auto-completion feature helps in avoiding spelling mistakes of keywords and makes code development easier. When the suggestion list highlights the statement of your choice, press Tab to use the keyword. The auto-complete feature does not interfere with your typing action. You can always press the Esc key to close the auto-completion list. However, as soon you start typing again and the keyword is known as a Uniface keyword, the list pops up again. There are three properties you can configure for the Code Completion feature within the SourceEdit definition inside the [widgets] section in the usys.ini file - Codecompletion , Codecompletionheight , and completiononenter as given below: Explain this code [widgets] SourceEdit=usrcedit(font=procboxfont;language=uniface;indenttabs=on;indentsize=4; indentguides=off;Codecompletion=true;Codecompletionheight=20;completiononenter=true) Copy You can switch off the code completion feature by setting Codecompletion=false . Note: By default the Codecompletion setting is set as True in the Uniface IDE. You can enhance the suggestion list using the Codecompletionheight property. By default, the suggestion list displays a maximum of ten visible items, and when needed, a scroll-bar is shown to indicate that there are more keywords than the visible items. You can scroll through the list using the up and down arrow keys. The list can show up to maximum twenty keywords, depending on the value set in the codecompletionheight property. Note: When the Codecompletionheight property is not defined, the default value is 10 . The minimum value for Codecompletionheight property is 10 . The maximum value for Codecompletionheight property is 30 . You can enable auto-completion with the Enter key using the completiononenter setting. The completiononenter setting is disabled by default. You can enable this setting with completiononenter=true . Table 1.
